rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Of Orders Of: Order Terminating Conservatorship On Death Of Conservatee; Settling And Approving Second And Final Account And Report Of Conservator; Allowing Conservator'S Fees And Attorneys' Fees And Costs And Reimbursement Of Costs Advanced
rulings.Rulings: The Motion for Reconsideration of Order Terminating Conservatorship on Death of Conservatee; Settling and Approving Second and Final Account and Report of Conservator; Allowing Conservator's Fees and Attorney's Fees and Costs and Reimbursement of Costs Advanced is granted in part and denied in part. The Motion is denied as to reimbursing the Conservator $1,162.50 for storage unit fees allegedly incurred for the Conservatee's benefit. A motion under CCP § 1008 must be based on "new or different facts, circumstances, or law." "The party seeking reconsideration must provide not just new evidence or different facts, but a satisfactory explanation for the failure to produce it at an earlier time." (Glade v. Glade (1995) 38 Cal. App. 4th 1441, 1457, citing Mink v. Sup. Ct. (1992) 2 Cal.App.4th 1338, 1342). The information presented as a basis for reconsideration consists of conservatorship bank records from 2018, and an undated email from the storage company showing the storage facility's charges for June through December 2018 and payments received from a Mastercard account held in the Conservator's name. There is no explanation as to why this information was not presented earlier. The July 17, 2019 hearing Minutes reflect that the Court asked the Conservator and his counsel about questionable credit card transactions, cash withdrawals, and transfers to other accounts, and that the Conservator responded "Yes" to the Court's question as to whether he should reimburse the conservatorship approximately $15,000, of which this surcharge amount is included. Conservator did not refer to or request an opportunity to present the information submitted with this motion. Conservator has not met the statutory standard for reconsideration of reimbursement of the storage unit fees under CCP § 1008. The Motion is granted as to the rescission of the $5,740 surcharge on transfers to a Wells Fargo account. This amount was authorized as conservator fees in the August 22, 2018 Order Approving First Report and Account of Conservator; etc. Conservator had ample opportunity to avoid this erroneous surcharge, and, again, failed to call attention to the prior order at the July 17 hearing. Nevertheless, the Court will not surcharge the Conservator for amounts it previously authorized to be paid as conservatorship fees. Conservator shall submit a proposed order consistent with this ruling. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. Wednesday, September 4, 2019.
